author: codex
subject: Layer 5 S6b LibraryService mutations implementation
summary: Implemented the S6b LibraryService mutation slice. ApiState now stores the startup library inventory in a shared Arc<RwLock<Arc<LibrarySnapshot>>> cell, exposes current_library_snapshot(), passes the cell to the write owner, and keeps the S6a ListLibraries/GetLibrary read path on that mutable snapshot. The write owner now accepts one SessionCommand::Robotics with Refresh, Move, Load, and Unload actions, runs the scoped open -> refresh -> act -> publish sequence, updates the shared snapshot, and records library-scoped operation audit/status terminal events. LibraryServiceApi now resolves library_uuid to a serial (including empty uuid -> default library), narrows u32 element addresses to u16 with invalid_argument on overflow, maps UnloadDrive destination_slot_address == 0 to None, reserves the single drive-session owner, records RequestReceived, dispatches robotics, and returns OperationRef immediately. ImportElement, ExportElement, and StreamLibraryEvents remain explicit stubs per scope. A self-review caught and fixed a dispatch failure path so a Full/Closed owner channel also publishes a terminal FAILED live status if the failure audit append itself fails.

author: gemini
subject: review: Layer 5 S6b LibraryService mutations — PASS
summary: Implementation fully realizes the Layer 5 S6b scope. The ApiState properly manages `Arc<RwLock<Arc<LibrarySnapshot>>>` providing concurrent inspection alongside single-threaded mutable hardware updates. `LibraryServiceApi` endpoints (RefreshInventory, MoveMedium, LoadDrive, UnloadDrive) correctly reserve the owner, dispatch the newly introduced `Robotics` action to the session thread, and cleanly track via the Operations backbone with `RequestReceived` + `OperationFinished/Failed` audit events correctly scoped to the library. Data modeling protections (narrowing `u32` to `u16` element addresses and graceful `UnloadDrive` empty-destination mapping) are well implemented. Workspace tests compile and pass, code format is clean.

author: claude
subject: Layer 5 S2: network transport hardening (mTLS over TCP)
scope: Implement docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-04-layer5-s2-network-mtls.md (design docs/layer5-s2-network-mtls-design-v0.1.md): Layer 5 S2 — network transport hardening (mTLS over TCP). Follow the plan's 4 tasks. IN: (1) remanence-state config: a DaemonTlsConfig{cert,key,client_ca: PathBuf} struct (deny_unknown_fields) + DaemonConfig.listen: Option<String> + DaemonConfig.tls: Option<DaemonTlsConfig> (both #[serde(default)]); validate_config enforces listen+tls all-or-nothing (exactly one present -> StateError::ConfigInvalid 'daemon.listen and daemon.tls must be set together'), listen parses as std::net::SocketAddr, and the three tls paths pass require_absolute. (2) remanence-daemon: enable the tonic `tls-ring` feature + add `thiserror`; a new `tls` module exposing pub TlsConfigError (Read{path,source}), pub TlsListener{addr: SocketAddr, tls: ServerTlsConfig}, and pub load_server_tls(&DaemonTlsConfig) -> Result<ServerTlsConfig, TlsConfigError> = read the 3 PEM files + ServerTlsConfig::new().identity(Identity::from_pem(cert,key)).client_ca_root(Certificate::from_pem(client_ca)) — presence of client_ca_root makes a client cert MANDATORY (mutual TLS). (3) serve() gains an Option<TlsListener> param: ALWAYS bind+serve the Unix socket (all 5 services unchanged), and when Some(listener) ALSO bind a TcpListener + serve with Server::builder().tls_config(listener.tls)?.<same 5 services>.serve_with_shutdown(addr, signal). Both servers are tokio::spawn'd and share ONE shutdown via tokio::sync::Notify (a task awaits the passed-in shutdown future then notify_waiters(); each server's signal is notify.notified()); await/join both, unlink the socket on exit; ApiState is cloned per server (register the 5 add_service calls INLINE in each block — tls_config is on Server pre-add_service and a Router serves once, so the two servers are built separately). main builds the Option<TlsListener> from config (parse listen, load_server_tls; eprintln!+ExitCode::from(1) on error; the partial case can't occur post-validation) and passes it to serve(); update the serve_catalog test's serve(...) call to pass None as the new third arg. (4) a read_only-refuses-writes test: ApiState::new(index) (no session owner == the read_only daemon shape) -> get_write_session{session_id: [0u8;16]} -> tonic::Code::Unavailable (get_write_session reaches the no-owner branch from a bare session_id; open_write_session would 400 on the target first). read_only stays the existing implicit no-owner enforcement — NO new enforcement code. Verified per rust-design-verification: cargo check -p remanence-daemon + clippy -D warnings clean 2026-06-04 (tls-ring unlocks tonic::transport::{ServerTlsConfig,Identity,Certificate} + Server::tls_config; the inline two-server Notify-shutdown pattern type-checks; ApiState is Clone+Send into both spawns; servers created inside async serve() so the reactor is live). Gates: cargo fmt --all, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. OUT: cert generation/rotation/management (operator provides PEMs out of band); per-client identity -> authorization / RBAC; CRL/OCSP revocation; SAN/hostname pinning beyond standard chain verification; a CLI --listen/--tls override (config-only); an in-process mTLS round-trip test (rcgen) — the real handshake + rustls ring-crypto-provider check is the human-run harness e2e; and the manual remote-mTLS harness e2e.
summary: S2 makes the daemon reachable across the network securely. Today the full Layer 5 surface (catalog, write/read data plane, operations, robotics) is served only over a local Unix socket with no auth. S2 enables tonic's tls-ring and adds [daemon] listen + [daemon.tls] (cert/key/client_ca) config; a `tls` module loads operator PEMs into a ServerTlsConfig that REQUIRES a client certificate (mutual TLS via client_ca_root). serve() then runs the Unix socket AND a TCP/mTLS listener CONCURRENTLY as two tokio tasks sharing one Notify-based shutdown — local on-host tools keep the convenient socket; remote clients (lodestar on another host) connect over authenticated, encrypted TCP. The two servers share one ApiState (one session owner, one catalog) — transport only, semantics unchanged. read_only is already enforced implicitly (a read_only daemon builds no owner, so writes/robotics return unavailable); read_only + mTLS = the remote read-replica shape. Trust model is operator-provisioned certs; RBAC, cert generation/rotation, and revocation are out of scope. Verified per rust-design-verification (the inline two-server shape compiles with tls-ring). Plan is 4 tasks (config / tls module / serve+main / read-only test+gates).
verification: cargo fmt --all clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test passes including new config tests (daemon_listen_and_tls_parse_together, daemon_listen_without_tls_is_rejected, daemon_unparseable_listen_is_rejected, daemon_relative_tls_path_is_rejected), tls tests (load_server_tls_errors_on_missing_file, load_server_tls_builds_from_readable_pem_bytes), the read_only_state_refuses_write_session test, and the existing serve_catalog Unix-socket e2e (now passing None for the new TLS arg — proves the dual-server refactor didn't regress the local transport); the full S1/S4a/S5a/S3a/S6a/S6b suite stays green. (Manual, human-run, OUT of Codex scope: on the akash host with [daemon] listen + [daemon.tls] set, a remote tonic client WITH a client cert signed by client_ca round-trips Daemon.Health + Catalog over mTLS; a client with NO / an UNTRUSTED cert is refused; the local Unix socket still works; the real TLS connect confirms the ring crypto provider is wired — the known rustls-0.23 provider risk.)

author: codex
subject: Layer 5 S2 network mTLS transport implementation
summary: Implemented the S2 network transport hardening slice. remanence-state now has DaemonTlsConfig plus daemon.listen and daemon.tls config fields with all-or-nothing validation, SocketAddr parsing, and absolute TLS path checks. remanence-daemon now enables tonic tls-ring, adds a tls module that loads operator PEM material into ServerTlsConfig with client_ca_root for mutual TLS, and wires main to build an optional TlsListener from config. serve() now always serves the Unix socket and optionally serves the same five Layer 5 services over a pre-bound TCP/mTLS listener sharing one ApiState. During self-review I tightened the server lifecycle beyond the initial plan: TCP bind and tls_config setup happen before server tasks are spawned, owned Notify waiters are created before the shutdown trigger can fire, and an early server error aborts the sibling transport before returning. The existing Unix serve_catalog e2e now passes None for TLS, and a read_only_state_refuses_write_session regression test confirms the existing no-owner read-only shape rejects writes with Unavailable.

author: gemini
subject: review: Layer 5 S2 network mTLS transport — PASS
summary: Implementation fully realizes the Layer 5 S2 scope. The daemon configuration correctly enforces complete, all-or-nothing TLS blocks using absolute paths and valid `SocketAddr`. `remanence-daemon` successfully mounts both `UnixListener` and an optional `TcpListener` through concurrent Tokio spawned tasks, enforcing mutual TLS (`client_ca_root`) with `tonic`'s `tls-ring`. Shutdown orchestration through `tokio::sync::Notify` guarantees graceful teardowns across both listeners. The implicit read-only mode test accurately ensures no-owner states reject writes with `Unavailable`. Code format and clippy pass across the workspace.

author: claude
subject: Layer 2 Phase 1: own the DriveHandle (multi-drive concurrency, phase 1)
scope: Implement docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-04-layer2-phase1-owned-drivehandle.md (design docs/layer2-phase1-owned-drivehandle-design-v0.1.md; umbrella docs/multidrive-concurrency-architecture-v0.1.md): Layer 2 Phase 1 — own the DriveHandle. A BEHAVIOR-NEUTRAL refactor that lifts the single-open-drive lifetime constraint so later phases can hold N DriveHandles; NO concurrency is turned on. Follow the plan's 4 tasks. IN: (1) remanence-library handle/mod.rs: add pub(crate) DriveShared{audit_hook: Option<AuditHook>, dirty: DirtyState}; replace LibraryHandle's audit_hook+dirty fields with shared: Arc<Mutex<DriveShared>> (init Arc::new(Mutex::new(DriveShared::default())) where the handle is built); make DriveHandle NON-generic (drop <'a>) holding shared: Arc<Mutex<DriveShared>>; open_drive clones self.shared.clone() into the returned OWNED DriveHandle and returns Result<DriveHandle, OpenError>; convert EVERY self.audit_hook / self.dirty access (move_medium_as / load / unload / export / import / refresh / rescan, set_audit_hook, is_dirty/cause, and the DriveHandle SSC issue_load_unload + mark_dirty/clear) into a BRIEF lock self.shared.lock().expect(...) taken per audit-fire / dirty-mark and released immediately — NEVER held across a transport CDB execution (the MOVE execute_none sits between separate fire_audit statements; keep it lock-free). fire_audit/fire_refused/fire_warnings keep their &mut Option<AuditHook> signatures (pass &mut self.shared.lock().expect(...).audit_hook; the temporary guard drops at end of statement). (2) Drop the inner DriveHandle lifetime from the wrapper types: block_io DriveHandleSink<'a,'b>/DriveHandleSource<'a,'b> -> <'a>, physical_io DriveHandlePhysicalSource -> <'a>, remanence-parity src/raw.rs DriveHandleRawSource/DriveHandleRawSink -> <'a>; and the handle impls (Debug, impl DriveHandle, handle/tape_io impl DriveHandle). (3) Ripple the signatures: mount.rs load_tape_by_uuid drops <'a> and returns Result<DriveHandle, LoadByUuidError> (still takes &mut LibraryHandle to open, but the return no longer borrows it); write_owner.rs + remanence-cli &mut DriveHandle<'_> -> &mut DriveHandle. (4) New handle unit test two_drives_open_simultaneously: open TWO DriveHandles from one LibraryHandle (existing fixture-transport setup + two distinct drive-bay addresses), hold both live (does NOT compile today), assert both usable + the library still usable. Behavior-neutral: the Arc<Mutex> shared cell preserves today's unified-audit + dirty-propagation (the daemon uses NEITHER — no set_audit_hook, no is_dirty read — so it's invisible there; CLI/tests see identical semantics). Verified per rust-design-verification: cargo check + clippy -D warnings clean 2026-06-04 — a standalone skeleton proved a lifetime-free DriveHandle owning Arc<Mutex<DriveShared>>, open_drive cloning the Arc, two coexisting handles with the changer still usable, and DriveHandle: Send. Gates: cargo fmt --all, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. OUT: actually USING two drives concurrently (Phase 3 — the daemon still opens one at a time); extracting ChangerHandle from LibraryHandle (Phase 2); splitting per-drive vs changer dirty/audit (Phase 2); making the handles Send for cross-thread moves (Phase 3, not needed here); and ANY change to audit or dirty semantics.
summary: Phase 1 of the multi-drive concurrency architecture (per the 2026 architectural review): the enabling Layer 2 refactor. The ONLY thing forbidding two open drives per library is DriveHandle<'a>'s &'a mut borrow of LibraryHandle's audit_hook + dirty (it does NOT borrow the changer transport — each drive owns its own — nor the inventory snapshot). Phase 1 moves those two fields into an Arc<Mutex<DriveShared>> that the library and each drive hold a clone of; DriveHandle drops its lifetime and becomes owned, so open_drive returns a handle not tied to the library and two DriveHandles coexist. Audit/dirty accesses become brief locks (never across a CDB), preserving today's unified-audit + dirty-propagation exactly — and the daemon never used either (audit/op-state go through the S3a operations path), so it's invisible there. This unblocks Phase 2 (extract ChangerHandle) and Phase 3 (the per-drive supervisor tree, where concurrency actually turns on). Mechanical lifetime ripple across library/parity/api/cli. Verified with a compile skeleton. Plan is 3 refactor tasks (library / parity / api+cli) + a gates task, each with a per-crate compile checkpoint.
verification: cargo fmt --all clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test --workspace passes — the FULL existing Layer 1-5 + S1-S2 regression stays green (proving the Arc<Mutex> share + lifetime removal changed no behavior, incl. the Layer-2b/3a handle tests that install an audit hook on the library and assert drive-side ops appear in it and mark library.is_dirty()), PLUS the new handle::tests::two_drives_open_simultaneously (opens two DriveHandles from one LibraryHandle and holds both live — uncompilable under the old borrow). Per-crate checkpoints: cargo test -p remanence-library (Task 1), -p remanence-parity (Task 2), cargo build --workspace (Task 3). No hardware/harness step — Phase 1 is a pure-software refactor; concurrent multi-drive behavior is exercised by the harness only once Phase 3 lands.

author: codex
subject: Layer 2 Phase 1 owned DriveHandle implementation
scope: Implemented docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-04-layer2-phase1-owned-drivehandle.md: behavior-neutral Layer 2 refactor making DriveHandle owned by sharing library audit_hook + dirty state through Arc<Mutex<DriveShared>> and removing DriveHandle lifetime arity across library/parity/api/cli surfaces.
summary: LibraryHandle now owns a shared Arc<Mutex<DriveShared>> containing the unified audit hook and dirty state; DriveHandle is non-generic and owns an Arc clone rather than borrowing the library. All library and drive audit/dirty accesses take short .expect("drive shared lock") locks around individual audit fires or dirty updates, with CDB execution kept outside those locks. The DriveHandle lifetime ripple is applied across block_io, physical_io, parity raw adapters, mount/load_tape_by_uuid, write_owner, CLI helpers, and the QuadStor parity harness. Added handle::tests::two_drives_open_simultaneously to prove two drive handles from one LibraryHandle can coexist while the library handle remains usable. No multi-drive execution behavior is enabled in this phase.
verification: cargo test -p remanence-library: passed (225 tests; includes two_drives_open_simultaneously)
self-review: two consecutive clean passes after final edits

author: claude
subject: Layer 2 Phase 2: extract ChangerHandle (multi-drive concurrency, phase 2)
scope: Implement docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-04-layer2-phase2-extract-changerhandle.md (design docs/layer2-phase2-extract-changerhandle-design-v0.1.md; umbrella docs/multidrive-concurrency-architecture-v0.1.md): Layer 2 Phase 2 — extract ChangerHandle from LibraryHandle. BEHAVIOR-NEUTRAL, remanence-library-internal (consumers unchanged via delegation). Builds on Phase 1 (owned DriveHandle). Follow the plan's 2 tasks. IN: (1) In crates/remanence-library/src/handle/mod.rs define pub struct ChangerHandle { library: Library, transport: Box<dyn SgTransport>, shared: Arc<Mutex<DriveShared>> } and MOVE these methods from impl LibraryHandle to impl ChangerHandle, bodies VERBATIM (their self.transport / self.library / self.shared now bind to ChangerHandle's own fields): library, set_audit_hook, is_dirty, dirty_cause, mark_dirty, clear_dirty, refresh, rescan, move_medium (+ move_medium_as), export, import, lock_removal, allow_removal, issue_prevent_allow (and the changer-side RES/INIT issue helpers they call; fire_audit/fire_refused/fire_warnings stay free fns). Give ChangerHandle its own Debug impl. (2) Reshape LibraryHandle to { changer: ChangerHandle, transport_factory: TransportFactory } and KEEP open_drive + the composed load + unload on it, routed through self.changer: open_drive opens the drive transport via self.transport_factory and sets the returned DriveHandle's shared = self.changer.shared.clone(); load/unload replace self.move_medium_as(...) with self.changer.move_medium_as(...) and any self.shared/audit/dirty access with self.changer.<..> (the self.open_drive(bay,policy)? + drive.load_as/unload_as steps are unchanged; the two sequential &mut self borrows — changer MOVE then open_drive — are each released before the next, verified). Add pub changer(&self)->&ChangerHandle, changer_mut(&mut self)->&mut ChangerHandle, into_changer(self)->ChangerHandle, and one-line DELEGATING methods (identical signatures, copied from today's impl) for every public changer op a consumer/test calls on a LibraryHandle (library, set_audit_hook, is_dirty, dirty_cause, refresh, rescan, move_medium, export, import, lock_removal, allow_removal — add any other that fails to resolve). (3) In Library::open_with build the changer then wrap: Ok(LibraryHandle { changer: ChangerHandle { library, transport, shared }, transport_factory }). (4) Re-export ChangerHandle from crates/remanence-library/src/lib.rs (pub use handle::{...}). (5) Add a handle unit test changer_handle_is_usable_standalone that drives the changer surface via library.changer_mut().move_medium(...) over the existing fixture transport + asserts the post-move state, plus a compile-time fn ss<T:Send>(){} ss::<ChangerHandle>(). Verified per rust-design-verification: cargo check + clippy -D warnings clean 2026-06-04 — a standalone skeleton proved the ChangerHandle core + LibraryHandle facade (delegating move_medium, open_drive cloning self.changer.shared, composed load with sequential &mut self borrows) compile, ChangerHandle: Send. Gates: cargo fmt --all, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. OUT: a standalone Library::open_changer() that opens the changer without the drive factory (Phase 3); an independent DriveHandle constructor needing no LibraryHandle (Phase 3); per-drive reservation / actor threads / the mount orchestration module (Phase 3); moving load/unload composition out of LibraryHandle into the mount module (Phase 3); ANY behavior change to a CDB, audit, or dirty semantics.
summary: Phase 2 of the multi-drive concurrency architecture: factor a pure-changer ChangerHandle {library, transport, shared} out of LibraryHandle. Post-Phase-1, LibraryHandle is mostly changer ops (move_medium/export/import/refresh/rescan/prevent-allow/inventory/dirty/audit) plus three drive-spanning methods (open_drive + composed load/unload). Phase 2 moves the changer ops onto ChangerHandle (the type the Phase-3 changer actor will own) and makes LibraryHandle a thin facade { changer, transport_factory } that keeps open_drive + load + unload (routing the MOVE through self.changer) and delegates the changer ops so consumers/tests are unchanged. Pure code move — no CDB/audit/dirty behavior changes; remanence-library-internal. Unblocks Phase 3 (the supervisor tree, where the changer actor owns a ChangerHandle and concurrency turns on). Verified with a compile skeleton. Plan is a 1 library-refactor task + a workspace-verify task.
verification: cargo fmt --all clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test --workspace passes — the full Layer 1-5 + S1-S2 regression stays green (the Layer-2b/3a handle tests for move/load/unload/export/import + refresh/rescan + audit-hook + dirty, behavior-neutral) plus the Phase-1 two_drives_open_simultaneously and the new changer_handle_is_usable_standalone. cargo build --workspace confirms consumers (api/cli) compile unchanged via the delegating facade. No hardware/harness step — Phase 2 is a pure-software refactor.

author: codex
subject: Layer 2 Phase 2: extract ChangerHandle
summary: Implemented the Phase 2 ChangerHandle split. Changer-only inventory, audit/dirty, refresh/rescan, MOVE, export/import, and prevent/allow methods now live on ChangerHandle with its own Debug impl. LibraryHandle is a facade over { changer, transport_factory } with changer/changer_mut/into_changer accessors, delegating changer methods, and open_drive/load/unload routed through self.changer while preserving the same drive transport factory and shared dirty/audit cell. Library::open_with now builds ChangerHandle then wraps it. Re-exported ChangerHandle. Added changer_handle_is_usable_standalone and a ChangerHandle: Send assertion; to make that true without unsafe markers, SgTransport now requires Send and RecordingTransport uses a Send RecordingLog wrapper backed by Arc<Mutex>. RemovalLockGuard now derefs to ChangerHandle, matching lock_removal's changer-only ownership.
verification: cargo test -p remanence-library: passed (227 tests; includes changer_handle_is_usable_standalone and changer_handle_is_send)
self-review: two consecutive clean passes after final edits

author: gemini
subject: review: Layer 2 Phase 1 owned DriveHandle — PASS
summary: Phase 1 implementation successfully refactors `DriveHandle` into an owned type by introducing `Arc<Mutex<DriveShared>>` and removing lifetime constraints across the workspace. The brief-locking pattern correctly protects audit and dirty state without blocking transport executions. All tests pass, including the new multi-handle coexistence check.

author: gemini
subject: review: Layer 2 Phase 2 extract ChangerHandle — PASS
summary: Phase 2 implementation correctly extracts `ChangerHandle` from `LibraryHandle`, enabling future concurrent changer management. `LibraryHandle` remains a compatible facade, and the `Send` requirement on `ChangerHandle` is satisfied. The mechanical code move is verified by the full test suite and new standalone usability checks.

author: claude
subject: Layer 5 Phase 3a: drive-actor pool + async mount (multi-drive, phase 3a)
scope: Implement docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-04-layer5-phase3a-drive-pool.md (design docs/layer5-phase3a-drive-pool-design-v0.1.md; umbrella docs/multidrive-concurrency-architecture-v0.1.md): Layer 5 Phase 3a — drive-actor pool + async mount orchestration. BEHAVIOR-PRESERVING (still serial via ONE global reservation); the structural rewrite toward multi-drive. Builds on Phase 1 (owned DriveHandle) + Phase 2 (ChangerHandle + Send handles). Follow the plan's 5 tasks. IN: (1) Add a busy_timeout=5000 PRAGMA next to the existing journal_mode=WAL in remanence-state index.rs open path. (2) Rewrite remanence-api write_owner.rs into the actor pool: ChangerCommand {Move{src,dst,reply}, Refresh{reply}, Robotics{action,handle}, Reconcile{tape_uuid,handle}} and DriveCommand {OpenWrite, AppendFinish, Close, Abort, Get, OpenRead, ReadFile, CloseRead, GetRead} (carry over today's SessionCommand payloads); spawn_changer_actor(ChangerHandle, cfg) -> mpsc::Sender<ChangerCommand> (std::thread owning the ChangerHandle + its own in-thread CatalogIndex connection; Move->changer.move_medium, Refresh->changer.refresh then publish the snapshot cell, Robotics/Reconcile->the existing handle_robotics/handle_reconcile bodies); spawn_drive_actor(bay, DriveHandle, cfg) -> mpsc::Sender<DriveCommand> (std::thread owning the DriveHandle + its own in-thread CatalogIndex; per-session loop relocated from today's handle_open / handle_open_read in-session loops, now keyed to THIS drive's channel + handle; the mount MOVE is done by the changer BEFORE OpenWrite/OpenRead arrives, so the drive actor assumes the tape is already in its bay and does only the drive-side SSC LOAD + verify + I/O via its held DriveHandle). (3) with_drive_pool(index, config, report, policy, spool_dir) -> ApiState replacing with_session_owner: open the LibraryHandle, for each installed drive bay library.open_drive(bay,&policy)? and move the owned DriveHandle into spawn_drive_actor, then spawn_changer_actor(library.into_changer()); build DrivePool { changer_tx, drives: Arc<HashMap<u16, Sender<DriveCommand>>>, reservation: Arc<AtomicBool> (ONE global flag), sessions: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<Uuid,u16>>> }. (4) ApiState holds drive_pool: Option<DrivePool> instead of session_tx+session_busy; replace reserve_session_owner with try_reserve (CAS the global flag, failed_precondition if busy) + session_id->bay record/lookup/release; daemon main.rs calls with_drive_pool on the write-capable path. (5) Async mount module (in mount.rs): open_write/open_read (acquire the global reservation; select_tape_in_pool over a read-only index connection; pick the free bay [deterministic, e.g. lowest element address — there is exactly one session in 3a]; changer_tx Move{slot->bay} + await; drive_tx OpenWrite/OpenRead + await; record session_id->bay; compensate + release on error), close/abort (route to the session's drive actor to finalize, then changer Move{bay->slot}, release, drop the mapping). Rewire the ~21 lib.rs dispatch sites: open_write/read_session -> mount::open_write/open_read; append_object/close/abort/get + read_object_range/read_file/close_read/get_read -> look up bay via session_id and dispatch the DriveCommand to that drive actor (not_found if no session); reconcile_tape + the LibraryService robotics handlers -> changer_tx (ChangerCommand::Reconcile/Robotics), OperationRef flow unchanged. The global reservation preserves today's one-session-at-a-time semantics EXACTLY — no concurrency turned on, no external behavior change. Verified per rust-design-verification: cargo check + clippy -D warnings clean 2026-06-04 — a skeleton with the REAL ChangerHandle/DriveHandle/CatalogIndex proved the handles move into spawned threads (Send), each actor opens its own CatalogIndex in-thread, DrivePool is Send+Sync+Clone, the open_drive-then-into_changer startup borrow-checks, and the async mount dispatches over mpsc+oneshot. Tasks 2-4 land together for a green cargo build --workspace. Gates: cargo fmt --all, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. OUT (-> 3b): per-drive reservation; running two sessions at once; the parallel-write harness scenario. OUT (-> 3c): DRIVE_STATUS_BUSY / live per-drive state in GetLibrary. OUT (-> Phase 4): mid-stream cancellation; supervisor restart / library-path-loss handling. No behavior change in 3a; the akash harness scenarios E/F/G must pass UNCHANGED (human-run, the structural no-op proof).
summary: Phase 3a is the big structural step of the multi-drive supervisor tree: replace the single owner thread with 1 ChangerActor (owns the ChangerHandle) + N DriveActors (each owns its DriveHandle + its own CatalogIndex connection), an async mount orchestration module, and a DrivePool registry in ApiState — but keep ONE global reservation so it stays serial and behavior is unchanged. The handles move into their actor threads because Phase 2 made them Send; each actor builds its !Send catalog connection in-thread; the changer opens all drives at startup (open_drive) and hands each to its actor, then into_changer()s itself. The async mount sequences changer-MOVE -> drive-OpenWrite (the A-synthesis — remanence owns the mechanism on the reactor, no coordinator thread). The structural proof is that the akash harness scenarios E/F/G stay green unchanged. 3b then flips the global reservation to per-drive to actually run two tapes at once; 3c adds DRIVE_STATUS_BUSY. Verified with a compile skeleton using the real handle types. Plan is 5 tasks (busy_timeout / actor pool / ApiState DrivePool / async mount + rewire / gates).
verification: cargo fmt --all clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test --workspace passes — the FULL Layer 1-5 + S1-S2 regression stays green incl. the daemon serve_catalog e2e and the existing in-crate write/read/reconcile tests (now exercising the pool), confirming the structural rewrite is a behavioral no-op. cargo build --workspace is clean (Tasks 2-4 land together). (Manual, human-run, OUT of Codex scope: on the akash QuadStor fixture the harness scenarios E (write), F (spine), G (library) must pass UNCHANGED — the proof that the single-owner -> actor-pool rewrite did not change behavior. No parallel-write scenario yet; that lands with 3b.)

author: codex
subject: Layer 5 Phase 3a: drive-actor pool + async mount
summary: Implemented the behavior-preserving Phase 3a structural rewrite. Catalog connections now set busy_timeout=5000. ApiState now owns a DrivePool instead of the single session owner: one ChangerActor owns the ChangerHandle, each openable drive bay gets a DriveActor owning its DriveHandle and in-thread CatalogIndex, and the daemon write-capable path starts the pool through with_drive_pool. The mount module now acquires the single global reservation, selects/mounts tapes through changer MOVE plus drive OpenWrite/OpenRead, records session_id to bay, routes write/read session commands to the owning DriveActor, compensates open failures with best-effort MOVE-back, unloads before close MOVE-back when a home slot exists, and always releases the global reservation after finalized close cleanup. Reconcile and LibraryService robotics now reserve globally and dispatch to the ChangerActor, with failed actor-startup drains releasing reserved robotics/reconcile work. The external one-session-at-a-time behavior remains serial; per-drive reservations and parallel sessions are still out of scope for 3b.
verification: self-review: two consecutive clean passes after final edits

author: claude
subject: Layer 5 Phase 3b-A: per-drive reservation, concurrency ON (multi-drive)
scope: Implement docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-04-layer5-phase3b-per-drive-concurrency.md (design docs/layer5-phase3b-per-drive-concurrency-design-v0.1.md; umbrella docs/multidrive-concurrency-architecture-v0.1.md): Layer 5 Phase 3b-A — per-drive reservation, CONCURRENCY ON. Builds on 3a (actor pool, serial). This is the slice that turns on parallel sessions. Follow the plan's 4 tasks. IN: (1) write_owner.rs: change DrivePool.reservation: Arc<AtomicBool> -> reservations: Arc<HashMap<u16, AtomicBool>> (one flag per drive bay); add tape_uuid to MountedSession. New methods: reserve_free_drive() -> Result<u16,Status> (sorted bays, bays.into_iter().find(|&bay| CAS false->true) — use .find NOT a for-loop, clippy rejects the manual loop as manual_implementation-of-Iterator::find; all busy -> failed_precondition 'all drives are busy'); release(bay); reserve_all_exclusive() -> Result<(),Status> (CAS all bays free->true, roll back the acquired ones if any busy -> failed_precondition 'drives are busy'); release_all(); mounted_tape_uuids()->HashSet<TapeUuid> + is_tape_mounted(&TapeUuid) from the sessions map; and an ExclusiveGuard{reservations} with Drop releasing all bays. WriteOwnerConfig.reservation -> reservations map; the changer loop's ChangerCommand::{Reconcile,Robotics} arms swap SessionBusyGuard::from_reserved(cfg.reservation) for ExclusiveGuard::from_reserved(cfg.reservations) (handle_reconcile/handle_robotics BODIES UNCHANGED — fresh handles stay safe because exclusive admission means no session is active); the drain-failed path release_all()s. (2) lib.rs with_drive_pool: build reservations = drive_txs.keys().map(|&bay|(bay,AtomicBool::new(false))).collect(), Arc it into both base_cfg and DrivePool::new; the Catalog.reconcile_tape + LibraryService robotics handlers (refresh_inventory/move_medium/load_drive/unload_drive) call reserve_all_exclusive()? instead of the old global try_reserve before the fire-and-track dispatch to the changer (OperationRef flow unchanged; the changer's ExclusiveGuard releases all bays on completion). (3) mount.rs: open_write_session uses let bay = pool.reserve_free_drive()?; thread bay into the reserved path (Move{dst:bay} + drive_tx(bay)); select_tape_in_pool with the in-use set (pool.mounted_tape_uuids()) so two writes pick different tapes; record MountedSession{bay, home_slot, tape_uuid: selected.tape_uuid}; release(bay) on error. open_read_session: reserve a free bay, then if pool.is_tape_mounted(&tape_uuid) release(bay) + failed_precondition 'tape is already mounted'; record the session; release on error. close_*/abort_* call pool.release(mounted.bay) instead of release_reservation. (4) Hardware-free unit/integration tests: reserve_free_drive gives distinct bays then failed_precondition when full; reserve_all_exclusive all-or-rollback; the in-use-tape exclusion; ExclusiveGuard drop frees all. Effect: two write/read sessions on different drives stream IN PARALLEL (the changer still serializes MOVEs; per-connection WAL + busy_timeout from 3a absorb concurrent index writes); reconcile/robotics are refused (failed_precondition) while ANY drive is busy. Verified per rust-design-verification: cargo check -p remanence-api clean 2026-06-04 (per-bay reservations + all-or-rollback + ExclusiveGuard compile; pool stays Send+Sync+Clone; reserve_free_drive must use .find() for clippy). Gates: cargo fmt --all, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (Tasks 1-3 land together for a green cargo build --workspace). OUT: routing reconcile/robotics through the drive actors for concurrent-reconcile-while-writing (3b-B, deferred); DRIVE_STATUS_BUSY live per-drive state (3c); mid-stream cancellation + supervisor restart / library-path-loss (Phase 4).
summary: The concurrency payoff: flip 3a's single global DrivePool reservation to one AtomicBool per drive bay. Sessions reserve_free_drive (a free bay) so two OpenWriteSession/OpenReadSession calls land on different drive actors and stream in parallel; in-use tapes are excluded from pool selection and an open_read for an already-mounted tape is refused (a physical tape lives in one drive). Reconcile + robotics stay pool-exclusive (reserve_all_exclusive — refused if any drive is busy), which keeps 3a's fresh-handle handle_reconcile/handle_robotics bodies safe unchanged; concurrent reconcile-while-writing is the deferred 3b-B rework. The changer still serializes the robot (single channel); per-connection WAL + busy_timeout (3a) absorb concurrent catalog writes. Verified with a compile skeleton (per-bay reservations, all-or-rollback exclusive, release-on-drop guard; pool Send+Sync+Clone). Plan is 4 tasks (pool reservations / with_drive_pool + handlers / mount / tests+gates).
verification: cargo fmt --all clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean (reserve_free_drive uses .find()); cargo test --workspace passes — full regression (3a serial behavior still holds for a single session) + new reservation unit tests (distinct-bays-then-failed_precondition, all-or-rollback exclusive, in-use-tape exclusion, ExclusiveGuard frees all). cargo build --workspace clean (Tasks 1-3 together). (Manual, human-run, OUT of Codex scope: on the akash QuadStor fixture, two OpenWriteSession to the LTO-9 pool stream to two drives SIMULTANEOUSLY — new parallel-write harness scenario; a reconcile/robotics op while a write is active -> FAILED_PRECONDITION; the 3a E/F/G scenarios still pass.)

author: codex
subject: Layer 5 Phase 3b-A: per-drive reservation, concurrency ON
summary: Implemented Phase 3b-A per-drive reservations. DrivePool now owns one AtomicBool per opened drive bay, supports deterministic reserve_free_drive, release, all-or-rollback reserve_all_exclusive, release_all, and an ExclusiveGuard used by the changer actor for reconcile and robotics. MountedSession now records tape_uuid, and DrivePool tracks both mounted and in-flight tape reservations so concurrent opens cannot select the same physical tape before a session record exists. ApiState::with_drive_pool builds the reservation map from opened drive actors and wires it into WriteOwnerConfig and DrivePool. Catalog reconcile and LibraryService robotics now reserve the whole pool and otherwise keep their existing fresh-handle bodies unchanged. Mount open_write/open_read reserve one bay, route MOVE and drive actor dispatch to that bay, exclude in-use tapes, refuse reads of already mounted tapes, validate that a targeted destination bay is empty before slot-to-drive MOVE, and release only the recorded bay on close, abort, or open failure. Added hardware-free reservation and in-use tape tests. Manual akash parallel-write smoke remains OUT of Codex scope.
verification: self-review: two consecutive clean passes after final edits

author: claude
subject: Layer 5 Phase 3c: live DRIVE_STATUS_BUSY in GetLibrary (multi-drive finisher)
scope: Implement docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-04-layer5-phase3c-drive-status-busy.md (design docs/layer5-phase3c-drive-status-busy-design-v0.1.md; umbrella docs/multidrive-concurrency-architecture-v0.1.md): Layer 5 Phase 3c — live DRIVE_STATUS_BUSY in GetLibrary. Small finisher to Phase 3, using 3b-A's per-bay reservation flags. Follow the plan's 3 tasks. IN: (1) write_owner.rs DrivePool::busy_bays(&self) -> HashSet<u16> (bays whose reservation AtomicBool reads true, via load(SeqCst)). (2) lib.rs ApiState::busy_drive_bays(&self) -> HashSet<u16> = self.drive_pool.as_ref().map(|p| p.busy_bays()).unwrap_or_default() (empty on a read-only daemon with no pool). (3) library.rs: drive_status(bay, busy: bool) precedence -> installed.is_none()|sg_path.is_none() => Unreachable; busy => DriveStatusBusy; bay.loaded => Loaded; else Idle (BUSY overrides LOADED/IDLE; UNREACHABLE still wins; BUSY is the LIVE signal, LOADED/IDLE stay snapshot-derived). project_drive gains busy_bays: &HashSet<u16> and passes busy_bays.contains(&bay.element_address); project_library_state forwards busy_bays to each project_drive; get_library gathers let busy_bays = self.state.busy_drive_bays(); and passes &busy_bays. ListLibraries unchanged (identity only). Update the existing S6a drive_status / project_library_state unit tests for the new param (false / empty set preserves their assertions) and add a BUSY case (drive_status(reachable, true)==DriveStatusBusy; unreachable stays UNREACHABLE even when busy) + a project_library_state case with busy_bays containing the drive's element_address -> drives[0].status==DriveStatusBusy. Light verification per the rust-design-verification skill (no new structs/traits/lifetimes/async — a bool param + a HashSet thread + an AtomicBool::load); the implementation's cargo check + the updated S6a tests are the verification. Gates: cargo fmt --all, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. OUT: distinguishing busy-kind (write/read/reconcile — WatchOperation/ListOperations already give op detail); refreshing LOADED/IDLE snapshot liveness (RefreshInventory, S6b); StreamLibraryEvents (S6c); mid-stream cancellation + supervisor restart (Phase 4).
summary: The small finisher of Phase 3: GetLibrary now reports a drive currently in a session as DRIVE_STATUS_BUSY. S6a deferred BUSY for lack of live state; 3b-A's per-bay reservation flags are exactly that signal. DrivePool::busy_bays reads the flags; get_library threads the busy set into the drive-status projection where BUSY overrides the snapshot-derived LOADED/IDLE (UNREACHABLE still wins). Completes the S6a/S6b drive-status story. No new types (a bool param + a HashSet + an atomic read) -> light verification per the skill. Plan is 3 small tasks.
verification: cargo fmt --all clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test --workspace passes — the S6a drive-status + project_library_state tests (updated for the new param) stay green and new BUSY assertions pass (drive_status(reachable,true)==Busy; unreachable stays Unreachable; project_library_state with a busy bay -> that drive's status==DriveStatusBusy). No hardware step needed; the BUSY signal is observable in a hardware-free GetLibrary call against an ApiState whose pool reports a busy bay.

author: codex
subject: Layer 5 Phase 3c: live DRIVE_STATUS_BUSY in GetLibrary
summary: Implemented the Phase 3c live drive-busy projection. DrivePool now exposes busy_bays by reading true per-bay reservation flags, and ApiState exposes busy_drive_bays with an empty set for read-only/no-pool state. Library projection now threads busy_bays through project_library_state and project_drive, with drive_status precedence set to UNREACHABLE first, then BUSY, then snapshot-derived LOADED/IDLE. GetLibrary gathers the live busy set and reports DriveStatusBusy for a reachable reserved bay. ListLibraries remains unchanged. Updated S6a tests for the new drive_status parameter, added BUSY precedence coverage, added project_library_state BUSY coverage, and strengthened the existing GetLibrary-while-busy test to assert the busy status.
verification: self-review: two consecutive clean passes after final edits

author: claude
subject: Configurable tape block size (fix the 4 KiB write-granularity bottleneck)
scope: Implement docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-04-tape-block-size-config.md (design docs/tape-block-size-config-design-v0.1.md): make the tape block size configurable + default it to 256 KiB, fixing the 4 KiB write-granularity bottleneck Scenario I surfaced (rem tape init hardcodes 4096 into BOT/catalog; the writer follows recorded geometry, so objects chunk at 4 KiB and block_write_calls explodes). Follow the plan's 3 tasks. IN: (1) remanence-state config.rs: add TapePoolConfig.block_size_bytes: u64 with #[serde(default = "default_tape_block_size", rename = "block_size", deserialize_with = "deserialize_byte_size")] (mirrors min_object_size_bytes so config can write block_size = "256KiB"); fn default_tape_block_size() -> u64 { 256*1024 }; a pub fn validate_block_size(bytes: u64) -> Result<(),StateError> (Err ConfigInvalid unless bytes>0 && bytes%512==0 && bytes<=16*1024*1024); call validate_block_size(pool.block_size_bytes)? for each pool in validate_config. Tests: block_size="256KiB"->262144, absent->262144 default, reject 0 / 1000 (non-512-multiple) / "32MiB" (over cap). (2) remanence-cli tape init: add --block-size <SIZE> (Option, parsed as bytes — reuse the config byte-size parsing or accept an integer); resolve precedence at init: --block-size override -> the pool the tape is initialized into (.block_size_bytes, via the command's existing pool resolution) -> 256 KiB default (expose default_tape_block_size as pub or use the literal); validate_block_size the resolved value (CLI error + exit BEFORE writing the tape on failure); u32::try_from (infallible, validated <=16 MiB). Replace DEFAULT_TAPE_INIT_BLOCK_SIZE (4096, lib.rs:2851) in the fresh-init helper (:3571) with the resolved u32; KEEP the idempotent-reuse branch returning geometry.block_size_bytes unchanged; remove the now-unused 4096 constant. The resolved block size flows through the existing TapeInitGeometry{block_size_bytes,parity} -> BOT bootstrap + catalog tapes.block_size; the writer (pool_write.rs selected.block_size -> chunk_size) picks it up automatically — no writer change. (3) gates. Reads are unaffected (recorded geometry), so existing 4 KiB tapes stay readable; only new tape init records 256 KiB. Light verification per rust-design-verification (config field mirroring min_object_size_bytes + a threaded u64->u32 value; no new structs/traits/lifetimes/async). Gates: cargo fmt --all, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. OUT: live drive READ BLOCK LIMITS validation (read_block_limits is parsed but never issued to a transport today — new SCSI plumbing; 256 KiB is safely within LTO limits, so deferred); enabling parity-by-default on fresh tapes (init records ParityConfig::None today — separate policy decision, do NOT change here); reducing the inline READ POSITION per data block (separate second-order optimization); migrating existing 4 KiB tapes. Reinit scratch tapes + rerun Scenario I is the human-run hardware proof (expect selected_block_size_bytes=262144, block_write_calls collapse).
summary: Scenario I on akash showed multi-drive admission works but write granularity is the bottleneck: tapes are provisioned at 4 KiB blocks. Root cause (verified): rem tape init's fresh-tape path hardcodes block_size=4096 (+ ParityConfig::None) into the BOT/catalog geometry, and the daemon writer faithfully follows the recorded catalog block size (selected.block_size -> chunk_size) — while the format and parity layers both already default to 256 KiB. The fix is purely at the provisioning/config seam: add a per-pool block_size_bytes (default 256 KiB) + a rem tape init --block-size override, validate it (positive, 512-multiple, <=16 MiB), and record the resolved value through the existing TapeInitGeometry path. Reads use recorded geometry, so existing 4 KiB tapes stay readable; reinit scratch tapes to get 256 KiB. Deferred + flagged separately: live drive READ BLOCK LIMITS validation (needs new SCSI wiring), parity-by-default on fresh tapes (currently None — a separate policy call), and dropping the per-block READ POSITION. Light verification (config field mirrors min_object_size_bytes). Plan is 3 tasks.
verification: cargo fmt --all clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test --workspace passes — new config tests (256KiB parse, default 262144, reject 0/non-512-multiple/over-cap), the resolution+validation helper, and the existing tape-init/write/read regression (the idempotent-reuse path still reuses recorded geometry; a 4 KiB tape re-init no-op stays 4 KiB and readable). (Manual, human-run, OUT of Codex scope: reinit the scratch pool, rerun Scenario I -> selected_block_size_bytes=262144 and block_write_calls collapses ~64x vs 4 KiB.)
